Maxwell avenue property owners
were before the board Of public
works this morning to protest against
the offer OF the Washington Water
Power company to restore the Max
we'll avenue grade providing the city
would stand half of the expense OF
raising the tracks and replacing the
grade.


To so cheeky proposition as that
advanced by Mr.. Richards and Mr..
Huntington the property owners
TREMENDOUSLY protest. They declare
the street was in perfect condition
until the Washington Water Power
company tore 1t up. cut ravines
Through the center, defaced the con-
tour and destroyed what was regard
I'd as a pretty driveway.


Commissioner Handley strongly
supports the property owners in their
protest and refuses to consider the
proposition Of forcing the city to
bay for half the damage done by the
btreet car autocrats.


.The Washington Water Power
company" said Mr.. Handley, has a


franchise worth a million and a half


md they got 1t for $4,000. It's queer


hat company cannot repair their
Iwn mistakes without asking the city


o help out on the expense. The


trset was cut down without author
~ and In violation Of the order of


he board Of public works. Now the
